summary:
Held 5 of today's 10 biggest US movers.

• 2 upside misses — ROIV +15%, RH +10%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 3 downside moves (HAS -8%, NTNX -7%, DUOL -6%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: ROIV (+15.40%) — Q4 FY2026 earnings (reported May 20): EPS $0.28 vs -$0.33 est (beat), driven by $770M Moderna patent-settlement gain and FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for brepocitinib in cutaneous sarcoidosis. Why we passed: Catalyst was a scheduled earnings release requiring pre-market positioning to capture the gap; news-reactive signals fire post-open after the bulk of the move was already realised. Revenue missed ($2.52M vs $3.82M est), creating mixed signals that likely tripped conservative dual-confirmation filters on earnings-day en

top_movers.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings (reported May 20): EPS $0.28 vs -$0.33 est (beat), driven by $770M Moderna patent-settlement gain and FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for brepocitinib in cutaneous sarcoidosis.
top_movers.miss_reason: Catalyst was a scheduled earnings release requiring pre-market positioning to capture the gap; news-reactive signals fire post-open after the bulk of the move was already realised. Revenue missed ($2.52M vs $3.82M est), creating mixed signals that likely tripped conservative dual-confirmation filters on earnings-day en
top_movers.suggestion: News-reactive strategy should add an earnings-beat gate that releases biotech names with EPS surprise ≥ +$0.50 and opening gap ≥ +10% within 5 minutes of market open, entering a scaled position at first-bar close rather than waiting for intraday signal confirmation.

top_movers.catalyst: US-China 90-day tariff truce expected to lift Enphase gross margins ~2pp, compounded by IQ9S-3P commercial microinverter pre-order launch and a $52M safe-harbor deal pushing backlog to $873M.
top_movers.miss_reason: ENPH had already surged ~32% in the prior week on the microinverter launch (May 14-15); by May 20 RSI was almost certainly deep in overbought territory (>80), tripping momentum-trader's RSI ceiling filter. The name looked like a laggard chaser, not a fresh signal, after that multi-day run.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a catalyst-continuation exception to momentum-trader's RSI filter: if a verified macro policy event (tariff truce) co-occurs with a product/backlog catalyst within the same 5-day window, allow RSI up to 85 for one entry session rather than the standard cutoff.

top_movers.catalyst: Unscheduled May 19 pre-open brokerage update reversed prior seasonal-decline guidance: May truckload gross profit per load now 'at least flat' vs. April; April volume beat Cass Freight Index by ~200bps.
top_movers.miss_reason: Stock had already rallied ~30% in two weeks (Q1 beat May 7 + Stifel upgrade May 15), likely tripping RSI-overbought filters on momentum-trader and mean-reverter; the unscheduled pre-open 8-K gapped price before news-reactive could establish an intraday position.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an unscheduled intra-quarter 8-K guidance-raise trigger to news-reactive: if GP/load or EBITDA outlook is revised upward outside a standard earnings window and open-gap is ≤15%, bypass the RSI ceiling and allow entry within 30 min of the open.

top_movers.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings beat: EPS $1.89 vs $1.48 est (+28%), revenue $151.9M vs $130.6M est (+16%); record TCE rates and irregular $1.00/share dividend declared.
top_movers.miss_reason: control-rule-based traded it, but momentum-trader and news-reactive almost certainly have an earnings-day blackout gate that blocks new entries to avoid gap risk — a standard guard that fires regardless of beat magnitude. Mean-reverter had no edge: a clean earnings gap-up is a breakout, not a fade setup.
top_movers.suggestion: Momentum-trader's earnings gate should release names where reported EPS beat ≥ 20% and post-open volume ≥ 1.5× 20-day avg within the first 30 minutes, allowing a trend-following entry on confirmed high-conviction earnings gaps.

top_movers.catalyst: WSTS projected 25%+ global semiconductor market growth in 2026; Intel's 22% data-center beat and Omdia forecast upgrade sparked broad sector buying, amplified by LSCC's bullish JP Morgan conference appearance on May 19.
top_movers.miss_reason: The driver was a sector-level macro signal (WSTS/Omdia forecasts + Intel read-through), not an LSCC-specific release, bypassing news-reactive's company-specific screener. Momentum-trader likely excluded the name via an overbought RSI or max-gap filter after LSCC's 50%+ YTD run entering the day.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a semiconductor-industry-forecast trigger to news-reactive: when WSTS or Omdia publishes an upgrade ≥+5 ppt to annual growth, release top-quartile-momentum names in the semis universe for entry within a 24 h window, subject to normal position-size limits.

top_movers.catalyst: Q1 EPS beat ($1.47 vs $1.26 est) but FY revenue guidance held at low-end (3-5% constant currency) and a March cyberattack delays $40-60M of Q2 consumer-products revenue to H2, spooking investors on the forward outlook.
top_movers.miss_reason: HAS reported pre-market on earnings day (May 20 call at 08:30 ET) — the earnings-gate filter almost certainly quarantined it from intraday strategies. The decline required parsing a mixed print (EPS beat + guidance disappointment + cyberattack drag), favouring pre-open positioning over post-signal entry; a post-gap sho
top_movers.suggestion: Earnings-gate should release names with a gap-down ≥5% at open on earnings day where FY guidance is maintained below consensus median, allowing news-reactive to enter within the first 30 minutes — but only if short capability is enabled; otherwise flag as '(no actionable change — requires short leg)'.

top_movers.catalyst: WTI crude fell >5% on reports Trump declared US-Iran talks in 'final stages', raising Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes; satellite data confirmed supertankers transiting the strait, signalling easing of the naval blockade.
top_movers.miss_reason: Commodity-momentum was likely long-only and held no USO position after the prior trend exhausted (crude had already spiked ~60% since Feb). The reversal was a sudden diplomatic catalyst—not a technical signal—so pre-session momentum filters saw no entry, and short capability would have been needed to profit.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a geopolitical-reversal exit rule: when a held commodity long has gained ≥40% over 60 sessions AND a monitored news-sentiment feed registers 'ceasefire/supply-restoration' keywords, flatten the position at open rather than waiting for momentum crossover.

top_movers.catalyst: Post-Q1 2026 earnings drift: Duolingo's May 4 report beat revenue ($292M vs $289M est.) but bookings guidance of +10–12% FY26 sharply disappointed; 'engagement-over-monetization' pivot and rising Treasury yields amplified the continued sell-off on May 20.
top_movers.miss_reason: The earnings catalyst was 16 days stale — outside news-reactive's lookback window. DUOL's confirmed -80% from-peak downtrend suppresses any long momentum signal. The partial bounce from $104 to $113 pre-May-20 erased the extreme oversold level that would trigger mean-reverter entry.
top_movers.suggestion: Extend news-reactive's earnings-overhang window to 20 sessions for names where reported bookings guidance delta is ≤ –15% vs. consensus; secondary post-guidance drift in that window is systematic and currently untraded.
